title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for submodules, nested repositories, and dependency boundaries and requires the smallest safe submodule dependency and trust manifest with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for submodule dependency and trust manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Gitlink Superproject Model evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Gitlink Superproject Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G59 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Gitlink Superproject Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Gitlink Superproject Model precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G59 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A submodule stores another repository and its full branch state inside the superproject commit.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Submodule Alternative Decision: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G66 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.
